```{r}
  mm <- mm[, colnames(mm) != "(Intercept)", drop = FALSE]

  # RF does not require scaling, but leaving it scaled is fine.
  # Keeping consistent preprocessing with glmnet for comparability.
  mm <- scale(mm)

  out <- data.frame(y = y, mm, check.names = FALSE)
  names(out)[1] <- outcome_col
  out
}

run_rf <- function(seed, pre_df, outcome_col, hp) {
  mikropml::run_ml(
    pre_df,
    method = "rf",
    outcome_colname = outcome_col,
    kfold = kfold,
    cv_times = cv_times,
    training_frac = 0.8,
    hyperparameters = hp,
    seed = seed,
    find_feature_importance = TRUE
  )
}

extract_performance <- function(model_list, dataset_name, outcome_name) {
  purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(model_list), function(i) {
    model_list[[i]]$performance %>%
      mutate(seed = i, method = "rf", dataset = dataset_name, outcome = outcome_name)
  })
}

extract_featimp <- function(model_list, dataset_name, outcome_name) {
  purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(model_list), function(i) {
    fi <- model_list[[i]]$feature_importance
    if (is.null(fi) || nrow(fi) == 0) return(NULL)
    fi %>% mutate(seed = i, method = "rf", dataset = dataset_name, outcome = outcome_name)
  })
}

# Load baseline NP + OP phyloseq and select sig ASVs
ps_np_t0 <- T0_np_ps_abs_filt
ps_op_t0 <- T0_op_ps_abs_filt

T0_np_ps_genus_abs_filt
T0_op_ps_genus_abs_filt
sig_np <- read.csv("../Output/Longitudinal/DA/MaAsLin2/T0_NP/ASV/T0_NP_ASV_delta_FEV1_pred.csv")$feature

op_sig_path <- "../Output/Longitudinal/DA/MaAsLin2/T0_OP/ASV/T0_OP_ASV_delta_FEV1_pred.csv"
sig_op <- if (file.exists(op_sig_path)) read.csv(op_sig_path)$feature else sig_np

ps_np_sig <- prune_taxa(sig_np, ps_np_t0)
ps_op_sig <- prune_taxa(sig_op, ps_op_t0)

# Build NP/OP feature tables
df_np <- ps_to_df_prefixed(ps_np_sig, prefix = "NP_", id_col = id_col)
df_op <- ps_to_df_prefixed(ps_op_sig, prefix = "OP_", id_col = id_col)

np_taxa_cols <- grep("^NP_", names(df_np), value = TRUE)
op_taxa_cols <- grep("^OP_", names(df_op), value = TRUE)

keep_common <- c(id_col, covars, outcomes)

# OP table: covars/outcomes + OP taxa
df_op_small <- df_op %>%
  select(all_of(intersect(keep_common, names(df_op))), all_of(op_taxa_cols)) %>%
  arrange(.data[[id_col]]) %>%
  distinct(.data[[id_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)

# NP table: covars/outcomes + NP taxa
df_np_small <- df_np %>%
  select(all_of(intersect(keep_common, names(df_np))), all_of(np_taxa_cols)) %>%
  arrange(.data[[id_col]]) %>%
  distinct(.data[[id_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)

# Combined: OP (with covars/outcomes) + NP taxa only (avoid duplicates)
df_np_taxa <- df_np %>%
  select(all_of(id_col), all_of(np_taxa_cols)) %>%
  arrange(.data[[id_col]]) %>%
  distinct(.data[[id_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)

m_comb <- inner_join(df_op_small, df_np_taxa, by = id_col)

m_np <- df_np_small
m_op <- df_op_small

datasets <- list(
  Combined = m_comb,
  NP = m_np,
  OP = m_op
)

# Train RF only across outcomes & datasets
all_perf <- list()
all_feat <- list()

for (outcome in outcomes) {
  for (ds_name in names(datasets)) {

    dat <- datasets[[ds_name]]

    taxa_cols <- switch(
      ds_name,
      Combined = c(grep("^OP_", names(dat), value = TRUE),
                   grep("^NP_", names(dat), value = TRUE)),
      NP = grep("^NP_", names(dat), value = TRUE),
      OP = grep("^OP_", names(dat), value = TRUE)
    )

    m_in <- dat %>%
      select(all_of(c(outcome, covars, taxa_cols))) %>%
      filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]])) %>%
      mutate(
        age_T0 = as.numeric(age_T0),
        gender_T0 = factor(gender_T0),
        smoke_status_T0 = factor(smoke_status_T0),
        season_microbiome_sampling_T0 = factor(season_microbiome_sampling_T0)
      )

    pre <- preprocess_safe(m_in, outcome_col = outcome)

    message("Running RF on ", ds_name, " outcome=", outcome)

    models <- future_map(
      1:seeds_n,
      ~ run_rf(.x, pre_df = pre, outcome_col = outcome, hp = hp_rf),
      .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
    )

    perf <- extract_performance(models, ds_name, outcome)
    all_perf[[paste(outcome, ds_name, "rf", sep = "_")]] <- perf

    readr::write_csv(perf, file.path(out_dir, glue("perf_{outcome}_{ds_name}_rf.csv")))
    saveRDS(models, file.path(out_dir, glue("models_{outcome}_{ds_name}_rf.rds")))

    fi <- extract_featimp(models, ds_name, outcome)
    all_feat[[paste(outcome, ds_name, "rf", sep = "_")]] <- fi
    if (!is.null(fi) && nrow(fi) > 0) {
      readr::write_csv(fi, file.path(out_dir, glue("rf_featimp_{outcome}_{ds_name}.csv")))
    }
  }
}

perf_all <- bind_rows(all_perf)
readr::write_csv(perf_all, file.path(out_dir, "perf_all_outcomes_all_datasets_RF.csv"))

feat_all <- bind_rows(all_feat)
if (!is.null(feat_all) && nrow(feat_all) > 0) {
  readr::write_csv(feat_all, file.path(out_dir, "featimp_all_outcomes_all_datasets_RF.csv"))
}


# Figures: RMSE and R²
# RMSE: CV vs Test
rmse_long <- perf_all %>%
  select(outcome, dataset, seed, any_of(c("cv_metric_RMSE", "RMSE"))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = any_of(c("cv_metric_RMSE", "RMSE")),
               names_to = "set", values_to = "RMSE") %>%
  mutate(set = recode(set, cv_metric_RMSE = "Cross-validation", RMSE = "Test"))

p_rmse <- ggplot(rmse_long, aes(set, RMSE)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.4, color = "red") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.4, color = "red") +
  facet_grid(outcome ~ dataset, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "RMSE", title = "RF performance (RMSE) across datasets") +
  theme_bw()

# Test R²
r2_long <- perf_all %>%
  select(outcome, dataset, seed, any_of("Rsquared")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Rsquared))

p_r2 <- ggplot(r2_long, aes(dataset, Rsquared)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.4, color = "red") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.4, color = "red") +
  facet_wrap(~outcome, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Test R²", title = "RF performance (Test R²) across datasets") +
  theme_bw()

p_rmse
p_r2


# Redone R2 plot 
# labels for outcomes
outcome_labs <- c(
  FEV1_pred_T1 = "FEV1",
  FVC_pred_T1 = "FVC",
  FEV1FVC_pred_T1 = "FEV1/FVC",
  MMEF_pred_T1 = "MMEF"
)

# long df for plotting
r2_long <- perf_all %>%
  dplyr::select(outcome, dataset, method, seed, any_of("Rsquared")) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(Rsquared)) %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = names(outcome_labs)),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NP", "OP", "Combined"))
  )

# performance summary for median labels per facet
perf_summary <- perf_all %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  summarise(
    test_R2_median = median(Rsquared, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = names(outcome_labs)),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NP", "OP", "Combined")),
    label = paste0("median R\u00B2 = ", sprintf("%.2f", test_R2_median))
  )

# RF R² plot (elastic-net style)
p_r2_rf <- ggplot(r2_long, aes(x = method, y = Rsquared, color = dataset, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.4) +
  # median label in top-left of each facet
  geom_text(
    data = perf_summary,
    aes(label = label),
    x = -Inf, y = Inf,
    hjust = -0.05, vjust = 2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.3,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  facet_grid2(
    outcome ~ dataset, scales = "free_y",
    labeller = labeller(outcome = outcome_labs),
    strip = strip_themed(
      background_x = list(
        NP = element_rect(fill = "#7987af", color = NA),
        OP = element_rect(fill = "#7c674d", color = NA),
        Combined = element_rect(fill = "grey40", color = NA)
      ),
      text_x = element_text(color = "white", face = "bold")
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(NP = "#7987af", OP = "#7c674d", Combined = "grey40")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c(NP = "#7987af", OP = "#7c674d", Combined = "grey40")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Test R²", title = "RF performance (Test R²) across datasets") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_r2_rf

# Identify best-performing (outcome x dataset) by median Test R²
summary_perf <- perf_all %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  summarise(
    n_splits = n(),
    test_R2_median   = median(Rsquared, na.rm = TRUE),
    test_R2_IQR_low  = quantile(Rsquared, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    test_R2_IQR_high = quantile(Rsquared, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    test_RMSE_median   = median(RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    test_RMSE_IQR_low  = quantile(RMSE, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    test_RMSE_IQR_high = quantile(RMSE, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    cv_RMSE_median   = median(cv_metric_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

overall_best <- summary_perf %>%
  arrange(desc(test_R2_median), test_RMSE_median) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1)

overall_best # Again the best model is for FVC % predicted and the NP variables

# Top features plot for a chosen outcome/dataset (RF importance)
outcome_plot <- "FVC_pred_T1"
dataset_plot <- "NP"      
top_n <- 15

# Read models + feature importance for this outcome/dataset
mods <- readRDS(file.path(out_dir, glue("models_{outcome_plot}_{dataset_plot}_rf.rds")))

# Extract feature importance per split
# Featrure importance is computed based on RMSE
get_rf_importance <- function(one_run) {
  fi <- one_run$feature_importance
  if (is.null(fi) || nrow(fi) == 0) return(NULL)

  # Your mikropml output uses: feat + perf_metric_diff (+ pvalue, lower, upper)
  if (!all(c("feat", "perf_metric_diff") %in% names(fi))) {
    stop("Unexpected feature_importance columns: ", paste(names(fi), collapse = ", "))
  }

  fi %>%
    transmute(
      feature = feat,
      importance = as.numeric(perf_metric_diff),
      pvalue = if ("pvalue" %in% names(fi)) pvalue else NA_real_,
      lower  = if ("lower"  %in% names(fi)) lower  else NA_real_,
      upper  = if ("upper"  %in% names(fi)) upper  else NA_real_,
      perf_metric = if ("perf_metric" %in% names(fi)) perf_metric else NA_character_,
      perf_metric_name = if ("perf_metric_name" %in% names(fi)) perf_metric_name else NA_character_
    )
}

imp_df <- map_dfr(mods, get_rf_importance, .id = "split")

#  Label helpers
get_niche <- function(x) {
  case_when(
    str_starts(x, "NP_") ~ "NP",
    str_starts(x, "OP_") ~ "OP",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  )
}

format_feature_label <- function(x) {

    if (str_detect(x, "^(NP_|OP_)")) {
    x2 <- str_remove(x, "^(NP_|OP_)")
    parts <- str_split(x2, "_", simplify = TRUE)
    parts <- parts[parts != ""]
    last <- parts[length(parts)]
    is_num <- str_detect(last, "^[0-9]+$")

    if (is_num && length(parts) >= 2) {
      tax <- paste(parts[-length(parts)], collapse = " ")
      return(paste0("<i>", tax, "</i> (", last, ")"))
    } else {
      tax <- str_replace_all(x2, "_", " ")
      return(paste0("<i>", tax, "</i>"))
    }
  }

  # non-taxa covariates / dummy vars: remove underscores, no italics
  str_replace_all(x, "_", " ")
}


# present as ΔRMSE = RMSE_perturbed - RMSE_original
# If your perf_metric_diff is the opposite (original - perturbed), flip the sign.
# Most often, negative values for "important" features implies it is original - perturbed.
imp_df2 <- imp_df %>%
  mutate(delta_RMSE = -importance)  # flip sign to become "worse when removed = positive"

# Summarise across splits + order by median ΔRMSE
imp_sum <- imp_df2 %>%
  group_by(feature) %>%
  summarise(
    med = median(delta_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    lo  = quantile(delta_RMSE, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    hi  = quantile(delta_RMSE, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(med))   # now descending matches x-axis (bigger ΔRMSE at top)

# imp_df2 is your split-level df with delta_RMSE
# (i.e. one row per split per feature)
imp_df_clean <- imp_df2 %>%
  mutate(feature_clean = case_when(
    str_detect(feature, "smoke_status_T0") ~ "Smoking status (baseline)",
    str_detect(feature, "season_microbiome_sampling_T0") ~ "Sampling season (baseline)",
    str_detect(feature, "gender_T0") ~ "Sex (baseline)",
    str_detect(feature, "age_T0") ~ "Age (baseline)",
    TRUE ~ feature
  )) %>%
  group_by(split, feature_clean) %>%
  summarise(delta_RMSE = sum(delta_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
# ^ sum is reasonable here because you are combining multiple dummy/contrast columns
#   that represent the SAME original variable within a split.

imp_sum_clean <- imp_df_clean %>%
  group_by(feature_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    med = median(delta_RMSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    lo  = quantile(delta_RMSE, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    hi  = quantile(delta_RMSE, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(med))

top_imp <- imp_sum_clean %>%
  slice_head(n = top_n) %>%
  mutate(
    niche = get_niche(feature_clean),
    label = map_chr(feature_clean, format_feature_label)
  ) %>%
  mutate(label = fct_reorder(label, med, .desc = TRUE))  # order matches x-axis

# Plot (ΔRMSE)
top_features_rf <- ggplot(top_imp, aes(x = med, y = label, color = niche)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lo, xmax = hi), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85, size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(NP = "#7987af", OP = "#7c674d", Other = "grey40"),
    breaks = c("NP", "OP", "Other")
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev) +   # <-- puts highest at the top
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = ggtext::element_markdown(),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    x = expression(Delta*"RMSE when feature perturbed (median ± 95% across splits)"),
    y = NULL,
    title = paste0("Top features by impact (RF): ", outcome_plot, " (", dataset_plot, ")")
  )

top_features_rf

# Save plots 
ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "Fig_RF_RMSE_all_outcomes.svg"), p_rmse, width = 4, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir, "Fig_RF_R2_all_outcomes.svg"), p_r2_rf, width = 4, height = 5)
ggsave(file.path(out_dir, glue("Fig_RF_top_features_{outcome_plot}_{dataset_plot}.svg")),
      top_features_rf, width = 6, height = 5)

```

### R2 plots from elastic net and RF
```{r}
# Combine R² plot: glmnet vs random forest in the same facets
outcome_labs <- c(
  FEV1_pred_T1 = "FEV1",
  FVC_pred_T1 = "FVC",
  FEV1FVC_pred_T1 = "FEV1/FVC",
  MMEF_pred_T1 = "MMEF"
)


perf_glmnet <- readr::read_csv("../Output/Longitudinal/Machine_learning/Elastic_net/perf_all_outcomes_all_datasets_GLMNET.csv") %>%
  mutate(model = "Elastic net")

perf_rf <- readr::read_csv("../Output/Longitudinal/Machine_learning/Random_forest/perf_all_outcomes_all_datasets_RF.csv") %>%
  mutate(model = "Random forest")

perf_both <- bind_rows(perf_glmnet, perf_rf) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Rsquared)) %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = names(outcome_labs)),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NP", "OP", "Combined")),
    model   = factor(model, levels = c("Elastic net", "Random forest"))
  )

# Summary medians per facet x model for annotation
annot_df <- perf_both %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset, model) %>%
  summarise(test_R2_median = median(Rsquared, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0(ifelse(model == "Elastic net", "EN", "RF"),
                   " median R\u00B2 = ", sprintf("%.2f", test_R2_median))
  ) %>%
  arrange(outcome, dataset, model) %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  summarise(label = paste(label, collapse = "\n"), .groups = "drop")

# Main plot
p_r2_both <- ggplot(perf_both, aes(x = model, y = Rsquared, color = dataset, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.25, width = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.35, size = 1) +
  geom_text(
    data = annot_df,
    aes(label = label),
    x = -Inf, y = Inf,
    hjust = -0.05, vjust = 1.5,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.3,
    color = "black",
    lineheight = 0.95
  ) +
  facet_grid2(
    outcome ~ dataset, scales = "free_y",
    labeller = labeller(outcome = outcome_labs),
    strip = strip_themed(
      background_x = list(
        NP = element_rect(fill = "#7987af", color = NA),
        OP = element_rect(fill = "#7c674d", color = NA),
        Combined = element_rect(fill = "grey40", color = NA)
      ),
      text_x = element_text(color = "white", face = "bold")
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(NP = "#7987af", OP = "#7c674d", Combined = "grey40")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c(NP = "#7987af", OP = "#7c674d", Combined = "grey40")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Test R²", title = "Performance (Test R²) across datasets") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)  )

p_r2_both

ggsave("../Output/Longitudinal/Machine_learning/Fig_R2_glmnet_vs_rf.svg", p_r2_both, width = 4.5, height = 5)

```
# Genus-level models 
## Elastic net and RF
```{r}
# Genus-level ML (EN + RF)
library(dplyr)
library(phyloseq)
library(glue)
library(readr)
library(purrr)
library(furrr)
library(tidyr)

plan("multisession")

out_dir_genus <- "../Output/Longitudinal/Machine_learning/Genus_level/"
dir.create(out_dir_genus, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

id_col <- "ID"
seeds_n  <- 10
cv_times <- 10
kfold    <- 5

covars   <- c("age_T0", "gender_T0", "smoke_status_T0", "season_microbiome_sampling_T0")
outcomes <- c("FEV1_pred_T1", "FVC_pred_T1", "FEV1FVC_pred_T1", "MMEF_pred_T1")

# --- reuse helper functions from your script (copy if not in env) ---
ps_to_df_prefixed <- function(ps, prefix, id_col) {
  otu <- as.data.frame(t(phyloseq::otu_table(ps)))
  meta <- as.data.frame(phyloseq::sample_data(ps))

  if (!identical(rownames(otu), rownames(meta))) {
    stop("Row names of OTU and metadata do not match; check phyloseq object integrity.")
  }

  colnames(otu) <- paste0(prefix, colnames(otu))
  df <- cbind(meta, otu)

  if (!id_col %in% colnames(df)) {
    stop(glue("ID column '{id_col}' not found in sample_data(). Available: {paste(colnames(df), collapse=', ')}"))
  }
  df
}

preprocess_safe <- function(dat, outcome_col) {
  dat <- as.data.frame(dat)
  y <- as.numeric(dat[[outcome_col]])
  x <- dat[, setdiff(names(dat), outcome_col), drop = FALSE]

  x <- x %>%
    mutate(
      age_T0 = as.numeric(age_T0),
      gender_T0 = factor(gender_T0),
      smoke_status_T0 = factor(smoke_status_T0),
      season_microbiome_sampling_T0 = factor(season_microbiome_sampling_T0)
    ) %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor))

  mm <- stats::model.matrix(~ ., data = x)
  mm <- mm[, colnames(mm) != "(Intercept)", drop = FALSE]
  mm <- scale(mm)

  out <- data.frame(y = y, mm, check.names = FALSE)
  names(out)[1] <- outcome_col
  out
}

run_glmnet <- function(seed, pre_df, outcome_col, hp) {
  mikropml::run_ml(
    pre_df,
    method = "glmnet",
    outcome_colname = outcome_col,
    kfold = kfold,
    cv_times = cv_times,
    training_frac = 0.8,
    hyperparameters = hp,
    seed = seed,
    find_feature_importance = TRUE
  )
}

run_rf <- function(seed, pre_df, outcome_col, hp) {
  mikropml::run_ml(
    pre_df,
    method = "rf",
    outcome_colname = outcome_col,
    kfold = kfold,
    cv_times = cv_times,
    training_frac = 0.8,
    hyperparameters = hp,
    seed = seed,
    find_feature_importance = TRUE
  )
}

extract_performance <- function(model_list, dataset_name, outcome_name, method_name) {
  purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(model_list), function(i) {
    model_list[[i]]$performance %>%
      mutate(seed = i, method = method_name, dataset = dataset_name, outcome = outcome_name)
  })
}

extract_featimp <- function(model_list, dataset_name, outcome_name, method_name) {
  purrr::map_dfr(seq_along(model_list), function(i) {
    fi <- model_list[[i]]$feature_importance
    if (is.null(fi) || nrow(fi) == 0) return(NULL)
    fi %>% mutate(seed = i, method = method_name, dataset = dataset_name, outcome = outcome_name)
  })
}

#  Load genus phyloseqs
ps_np_genus_t0 <- T0_np_ps_genus_abs_filt
ps_op_genus_t0 <- T0_op_ps_genus_abs_filt


# Restrict to "significant genera" (MaAsLin2 Genus)
ps_np_genus_t0 <- T0_np_ps_genus_abs_filt
ps_op_genus_t0 <- T0_op_ps_genus_abs_filt

# Choose which outcome's MaAsLin2 results define "significant"
# (mirrors your ASV strategy; change if you prefer another outcome)
sig_outcome <- "delta_FEV1_pred"

# Paths to genus-level MaAsLin2 results
sig_np_path <- file.path(
  "../Output/Longitudinal/DA/MaAsLin2/T0_NP/Genus",
  paste0("T0_NP_Genus_", sig_outcome, ".csv")
)

sig_op_path <- file.path(
  "../Output/Longitudinal/DA/MaAsLin2/T0_OP/Genus",
  paste0("T0_OP_Genus_", sig_outcome, ".csv")
)

# Read significant genera lists
sig_np_genus <- readr::read_csv(sig_np_path, show_col_types = FALSE)$feature

sig_op_genus <- if (file.exists(sig_op_path)) {
  readr::read_csv(sig_op_path, show_col_types = FALSE)$feature
} else {
  sig_np_genus
}

# (Optional but recommended) keep only taxa actually present in each phyloseq
sig_np_genus <- intersect(sig_np_genus, taxa_names(ps_np_genus_t0))
sig_op_genus <- intersect(sig_op_genus, taxa_names(ps_op_genus_t0))

# Prune genus phyloseqs to significant genera
ps_np_genus_sig <- phyloseq::prune_taxa(sig_np_genus, ps_np_genus_t0)
ps_op_genus_sig <- phyloseq::prune_taxa(sig_op_genus, ps_op_genus_t0)

# Build NP/OP genus feature tables from the pruned objects
df_np_g <- ps_to_df_prefixed(ps_np_genus_sig, prefix = "NPg_", id_col = id_col)
df_op_g <- ps_to_df_prefixed(ps_op_genus_sig, prefix = "OPg_", id_col = id_col)

np_taxa_cols <- grep("^NPg_", names(df_np_g), value = TRUE)
op_taxa_cols <- grep("^OPg_", names(df_op_g), value = TRUE)




keep_common <- c(id_col, covars, outcomes)

df_op_small <- df_op_g %>%
  select(all_of(intersect(keep_common, names(df_op_g))), all_of(op_taxa_cols)) %>%
  arrange(.data[[id_col]]) %>%
  distinct(.data[[id_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)

df_np_small <- df_np_g %>%
  select(all_of(intersect(keep_common, names(df_np_g))), all_of(np_taxa_cols)) %>%
  arrange(.data[[id_col]]) %>%
  distinct(.data[[id_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)

df_np_taxa <- df_np_g %>%
  select(all_of(id_col), all_of(np_taxa_cols)) %>%
  arrange(.data[[id_col]]) %>%
  distinct(.data[[id_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)

m_comb <- inner_join(df_op_small, df_np_taxa, by = id_col)

datasets <- list(Combined = m_comb, NP = df_np_small, OP = df_op_small)

# Hyperparameter grids (match your ASV analysis)
hp_glmnet <- list(alpha = c(0, 0.5, 1), lambda = c(0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05))
hp_rf     <- list(mtry = c(2, 4, 6))

# ---- Run both methods ----
all_perf <- list()
all_feat <- list()

for (outcome in outcomes) {
  for (ds_name in names(datasets)) {

    dat <- datasets[[ds_name]]

    taxa_cols <- switch(
      ds_name,
      Combined = c(grep("^OPg_", names(dat), value = TRUE),
                   grep("^NPg_", names(dat), value = TRUE)),
      NP = grep("^NPg_", names(dat), value = TRUE),
      OP = grep("^OPg_", names(dat), value = TRUE)
    )

    m_in <- dat %>%
      select(all_of(c(outcome, covars, taxa_cols))) %>%
      filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]]))

    pre <- preprocess_safe(m_in, outcome_col = outcome)

    # --- Elastic net ---
    message("[GENUS] Running glmnet on ", ds_name, " outcome=", outcome)
    models_en <- future_map(
      1:seeds_n,
      ~ run_glmnet(.x, pre_df = pre, outcome_col = outcome, hp = hp_glmnet),
      .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
    )

    perf_en <- extract_performance(models_en, ds_name, outcome, "glmnet_genus")
    all_perf[[paste(outcome, ds_name, "glmnet_genus", sep = "_")]] <- perf_en
    saveRDS(models_en, file.path(out_dir_genus, glue("models_{outcome}_{ds_name}_glmnet_genus.rds")))
    write_csv(perf_en, file.path(out_dir_genus, glue("perf_{outcome}_{ds_name}_glmnet_genus.csv")))

    fi_en <- extract_featimp(models_en, ds_name, outcome, "glmnet_genus")
    all_feat[[paste(outcome, ds_name, "glmnet_genus", sep = "_")]] <- fi_en
    if (!is.null(fi_en) && nrow(fi_en) > 0) {
      write_csv(fi_en, file.path(out_dir_genus, glue("featimp_{outcome}_{ds_name}_glmnet_genus.csv")))
    }

    # --- Random forest ---
    message("[GENUS] Running RF on ", ds_name, " outcome=", outcome)
    models_rf <- future_map(
      1:seeds_n,
      ~ run_rf(.x, pre_df = pre, outcome_col = outcome, hp = hp_rf),
      .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)
    )

    perf_rf <- extract_performance(models_rf, ds_name, outcome, "rf_genus")
    all_perf[[paste(outcome, ds_name, "rf_genus", sep = "_")]] <- perf_rf
    saveRDS(models_rf, file.path(out_dir_genus, glue("models_{outcome}_{ds_name}_rf_genus.rds")))
    write_csv(perf_rf, file.path(out_dir_genus, glue("perf_{outcome}_{ds_name}_rf_genus.csv")))

    fi_rf <- extract_featimp(models_rf, ds_name, outcome, "rf_genus")
    all_feat[[paste(outcome, ds_name, "rf_genus", sep = "_")]] <- fi_rf
    if (!is.null(fi_rf) && nrow(fi_rf) > 0) {
      write_csv(fi_rf, file.path(out_dir_genus, glue("featimp_{outcome}_{ds_name}_rf_genus.csv")))
    }
  }
}

perf_all_genus <- bind_rows(all_perf)
write_csv(perf_all_genus, file.path(out_dir_genus, "perf_all_outcomes_all_datasets_GENUS.csv"))

feat_all_genus <- bind_rows(all_feat)
if (!is.null(feat_all_genus) && nrow(feat_all_genus) > 0) {
  write_csv(feat_all_genus, file.path(out_dir_genus, "featimp_all_outcomes_all_datasets_GENUS.csv"))
}

# Quick summary table (median test R2)
perf_summary_genus <- perf_all_genus %>%
  group_by(method, outcome, dataset) %>%
  summarise(test_R2_median = median(Rsquared, na.rm = TRUE), n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(method, outcome, desc(test_R2_median))

perf_summary_genus
write_csv(perf_summary_genus, file.path(out_dir_genus, "perf_summary_GENUS.csv"))
```
## R2 plots from genus-level EN and RF models
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggh4x)

# Combine R² plot: glmnet vs random forest (GENUS level)
outcome_labs <- c(
  FEV1_pred_T1 = "FEV1",
  FVC_pred_T1 = "FVC",
  FEV1FVC_pred_T1 = "FEV1/FVC",
  MMEF_pred_T1 = "MMEF"
)

# Read genus-level performance (from your genus ML chunk outputs)
perf_genus <- readr::read_csv(
  "../Output/Longitudinal/Machine_learning/Genus_level/perf_all_outcomes_all_datasets_GENUS.csv",
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(
    model = case_when(
      grepl("glmnet", method) ~ "Elastic net",
      grepl("rf", method)     ~ "Random forest",
      TRUE ~ as.character(method)
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Rsquared)) %>%
  mutate(
    outcome = factor(outcome, levels = names(outcome_labs)),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("NP", "OP", "Combined")),
    model   = factor(model, levels = c("Elastic net", "Random forest"))
  )

# Summary medians per facet x model for annotation
annot_df <- perf_genus %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset, model) %>%
  summarise(test_R2_median = median(Rsquared, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    label = paste0(ifelse(model == "Elastic net", "EN", "RF"),
                   " median R\u00B2 = ", sprintf("%.2f", test_R2_median))
  ) %>%
  arrange(outcome, dataset, model) %>%
  group_by(outcome, dataset) %>%
  summarise(label = paste(label, collapse = "\n"), .groups = "drop")

# Main plot (same styling as ASV plot)
p_r2_genus <- ggplot(perf_genus, aes(x = model, y = Rsquared, color = dataset, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.25, width = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.35, size = 1) +
  geom_text(
    data = annot_df,
    aes(label = label),
    x = -Inf, y = Inf,
    hjust = -0.05, vjust = 1.5,
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.3,
    color = "black",
    lineheight = 0.95
  ) +
  facet_grid2(
    outcome ~ dataset, scales = "free_y",
    labeller = labeller(outcome = outcome_labs),
    strip = strip_themed(
      background_x = list(
        NP = element_rect(fill = "#7987af", color = NA),
        OP = element_rect(fill = "#7c674d", color = NA),
        Combined = element_rect(fill = "grey40", color = NA)
      ),
      text_x = element_text(color = "white", face = "bold")
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(NP = "#7987af", OP = "#7c674d", Combined = "grey40")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c(NP = "#7987af", OP = "#7c674d", Combined = "grey40")) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Test R²", title = "Genus-level performance (Test R²) across datasets") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)
  )

p_r2_genus

ggsave("../Output/Longitudinal/Machine_learning/Fig_R2_glmnet_vs_rf_GENUS.svg",
       p_r2_genus, width = 4.5, height = 5)
```

# Performance of asv vs genus level models
```{r}
# Compare ASV vs Genus model performance

ml_root <- "../Output/Longitudinal/Machine_learning"

# ---------------------------
# Load ASV performance
# ---------------------------
perf_asv_glmnet <- read_csv(
  file.path(ml_root,
            "Elastic_net/perf_all_outcomes_all_datasets_GLMNET.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(model = "Elastic net", tax_level = "ASV")

perf_asv_rf <- read_csv(
  file.path(ml_root,
            "Random_forest/perf_all_outcomes_all_datasets_RF.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(model = "Random forest", tax_level = "ASV")

perf_asv <- bind_rows(perf_asv_glmnet, perf_asv_rf)

# ---------------------------
# Load GENUS performance
# ---------------------------
perf_genus <- read_csv(
  file.path(ml_root,
            "Genus_level/perf_all_outcomes_all_datasets_GENUS.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(
    model = case_when(
      grepl("glmnet", method) ~ "Elastic net",
      grepl("rf", method) ~ "Random forest"
    ),
    tax_level = "Genus"
  )

# ---------------------------
# Combine
# ---------------------------
perf_compare <- bind_rows(perf_asv, perf_genus) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Rsquared))

# ---------------------------
# Summary table (decision table)
# ---------------------------
perf_summary_compare <- perf_compare %>%
  group_by(tax_level, model, outcome, dataset) %>%
  summarise(
    median_R2 = median(Rsquared, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_low = quantile(Rsquared, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_high = quantile(Rsquared, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(outcome, dataset, desc(median_R2))

perf_summary_compare

write_csv(
  perf_summary_compare,
  file.path(ml_root, "ASV_vs_Genus_R2_summary.csv")
)

# ---------------------------
# Overall winner per outcome
# ---------------------------
winner_table <- perf_summary_compare %>%
  group_by(model, outcome, dataset) %>%
  slice_max(median_R2, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

winner_table

# ---------------------------
# Visualization
# ---------------------------
outcome_labs <- c(
  FEV1_pred_T1 = "FEV1",
  FVC_pred_T1 = "FVC",
  FEV1FVC_pred_T1 = "FEV1/FVC",
  MMEF_pred_T1 = "MMEF"
)

p_compare <- ggplot(
  perf_compare,
  aes(x = tax_level, y = Rsquared,
      fill = tax_level, color = tax_level)
) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.35, size = 1) +
  facet_grid(model ~ dataset + outcome,
             labeller = labeller(outcome = outcome_labs)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(ASV = "#4C72B0", Genus = "#DD8452")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(ASV = "#4C72B0", Genus = "#DD8452")) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Test R²",
    title = "Predictive performance: ASV vs Genus models"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_compare

ggsave(
  file.path(ml_root, "Fig_ASV_vs_Genus_performance.svg"),
  p_compare,
  width = 10,
  height = 6
)
```


# Microbiome change over time 
# Beta diversity
## Data
```{r}
ps_RQ2_pairedOnly <- readRDS("../Output/Longitudinal/Phyloseq/ps_RQ2_pairedNiche_only.rds")
ps_RQ2_pairedOnly
# phyloseq-class experiment-level object
# otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 7881 taxa and 312 samples ]
# sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 312 samples by 85 sample variables ]
# tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 7881 taxa by 9 taxonomic ranks ]
# refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 7881 reference sequences ]

length(unique(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly@sam_data$ID))# 108
```
# Count ASVs, phyla, class, order etc. in dataset
```{r}
ps_RQ2_pairedOnly
ntaxa(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly)

# Extract taxonomy table
tax_df_paired <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly))
colnames(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly@tax_table)

# Count taxa at each rank
tax_counts <- list(
  ASVs    = ntaxa(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly),
  Phylum  = n_distinct(na.omit(tax_df_paired$phylum)),
  Class   = n_distinct(na.omit(tax_df_paired$class)),
  Order   = n_distinct(na.omit(tax_df_paired$order)),
  Family  = n_distinct(na.omit(tax_df_paired$family)),
  Genus   = n_distinct(na.omit(tax_df_paired$genus)),
  Species = n_distinct(na.omit(tax_df_paired$species))
)

tax_counts
# $ASVs
# [1] 7881
# 
# $Phylum
# [1] 36
# 
# $Class
# [1] 86
# 
# $Order
# [1] 194
# 
# $Family
# [1] 326
# 
# $Genus
# [1] 844
# 
# $Species
# [1] 790
```


## NP within vs between individual composition
```{r}
id_var <- "ID"

# Subset & merge (fix the typo) 
VGO2_NP_ps <- subset_samples(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly, study == "VGO2" & niche == "NP")
VGO3_NP_ps <- subset_samples(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly, study == "VGO3" & niche == "NP") 
VGO_NP_combined <- merge_phyloseq(VGO2_NP_ps, VGO3_NP_ps)

# Filter & RA, then Bray–Curtis 
VGO_NP_RA <- VGO_NP_combined %>% pres_abund_filter() %>% to_RA()
# A total of 481 ASVs were found to be present at or above a level of confident detection (0.1% relative abundance) in at least 2 samples (n = 7400 ASVs excluded).
np_dist   <- phyloseq::distance(VGO_NP_RA, method = "bray")
meta_np   <- sample_data(VGO_NP_RA) %>% data.frame(check.names = FALSE)

# How many participants have NP samples at BOTH time points (i.e., >=2 NP samples)?
paired_ids_np <- meta_np %>%
  dplyr::count(.data[[id_var]]) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n >= 2)

n_paired_individuals_np <- nrow(paired_ids_np)
n_paired_individuals_np # 84

# Long pairwise distances + subject IDs 
np_pairs <- as.matrix(np_dist) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Sample1") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Sample1, names_to = "Sample2", values_to = "Distance") %>%
  dplyr::filter(Sample1 < Sample2) %>%                        # unique pairs
  dplyr::mutate(
    ID1 = meta_np[Sample1, id_var],
    ID2 = meta_np[Sample2, id_var]
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(ID1) & !is.na(ID2)) %>%                # drop NA IDs
  dplyr::mutate(Comparison = ifelse(ID1 == ID2, "Within individual", "Between individuals"))

# Sanity check: need both groups 
if (!all(c("Within individual","Between individuals") %in% unique(np_pairs$Comparison))) {
  stop("You don't have both 'Within' and 'Between' pairs. Check that VGO2 & VGO3 NP samples exist and IDs are populated.")
}

# Balance the groups (downsample larger to the size of the smaller)
set.seed(123)
n_to_sample <- min(table(np_pairs$Comparison))
np_bal <- np_pairs %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Comparison) %>%
  dplyr::slice_sample(n = n_to_sample) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Y limits from boxplot whiskers (avoid NA)
whisk <- np_bal %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Comparison) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    whisker_low  = boxplot.stats(Distance)$stats[1],
    whisker_high = boxplot.stats(Distance)$stats[5],
    .groups = "drop"
  )

y_min <- min(whisk$whisker_low,  na.rm = TRUE)
y_max <- min(1, max(whisk$whisker_high, na.rm = TRUE))
range_y <- y_max - y_min
# add generous space for bracket + label
y_max_ext <- y_max + 0.12 * range_y
# bracket position
p_y <- y_max + 0.05 * range_y


# Test & label
wt <- wilcox.test(Distance ~ Comparison, data = np_bal, exact = FALSE)
label <- ifelse(is.finite(wt$p.value) && wt$p.value < 0.001,
                "italic(p) < 0.001",
                paste0("italic(p) == ", sprintf('%.3f', wt$p.value)))

# Plot
NP_plot <- ggplot(np_bal, aes(x = Comparison, y = Distance, fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Comparison), width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Within individual" = "#7987af", "Between individuals" = "#D2D7E4")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Within individual" = "#7987af", "Between individuals" = "#D2D7E4")) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(y_min, y_max_ext), clip = "off") +
  labs(y = "Bray–Curtis Dissimilarity") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  ggsignif::geom_signif(
    annotations = label, y_position = p_y,
    xmin = 1, xmax = 2, tip_length = 0.01, parse = TRUE
  )

ggsave("../Output/Longitudinal/Beta_diversity/NP_betweenvswithin_BC.svg",
       NP_plot, width = 5, height = 5)

```

## OP within vs between individual composition
```{r}
id_var <- "ID"

# Subset & merge (fix the typo) 
VGO2_OP_ps <- subset_samples(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly, study == "VGO2" & niche == "OP")
VGO3_OP_ps <- subset_samples(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly, study == "VGO3" & niche == "OP") 
VGO_OP_combined <- merge_phyloseq(VGO2_OP_ps, VGO3_OP_ps)

# Filter & RA, then Bray–Curtis 
VGO_OP_RA <- VGO_OP_combined %>% pres_abund_filter() %>% to_RA()
# A total of 481 ASVs were found to be present at or above a level of confident detection (0.1% relative abundance) in at least 2 samples (n = 7400 ASVs excluded).
OP_dist   <- phyloseq::distance(VGO_OP_RA, method = "bray")
meta_OP   <- sample_data(VGO_OP_RA) %>% data.frame(check.names = FALSE)

# How many participants have NP samples at BOTH time points (i.e., >=2 OP samples)?
paired_ids_op <- meta_OP %>%
  dplyr::count(.data[[id_var]]) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n >= 2)

n_paired_individuals_op <- nrow(paired_ids_op)
n_paired_individuals_op # 72

# Long pairwise distances + subject IDs 
OP_pairs <- as.matrix(OP_dist) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Sample1") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(-Sample1, names_to = "Sample2", values_to = "Distance") %>%
  dplyr::filter(Sample1 < Sample2) %>%                        # unique pairs
  dplyr::mutate(
    ID1 = meta_OP[Sample1, id_var],
    ID2 = meta_OP[Sample2, id_var]
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(ID1) & !is.na(ID2)) %>%                # drop NA IDs
  dplyr::mutate(Comparison = ifelse(ID1 == ID2, "Within individual", "Between individuals"))

# Sanity check: need both groups 
if (!all(c("Within individual","Between individuals") %in% unique(OP_pairs$Comparison))) {
  stop("You don't have both 'Within' and 'Between' pairs. Check that VGO2 & VGO3 OP samples exist and IDs are populated.")
}

# Balance the groups (downsample larger to the size of the smaller)
set.seed(123)
n_to_sample <- min(table(OP_pairs$Comparison))
OP_bal <- OP_pairs %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Comparison) %>%
  dplyr::slice_sample(n = n_to_sample) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# Y limits from boxplot whiskers (avoid NA)
whisk <- OP_bal %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Comparison) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    whisker_low  = boxplot.stats(Distance)$stats[1],
    whisker_high = boxplot.stats(Distance)$stats[5],
    .groups = "drop"
  )

y_min <- min(whisk$whisker_low,  na.rm = TRUE)
y_max <- min(1, max(whisk$whisker_high, na.rm = TRUE))
range_y <- y_max - y_min
# add generous space for bracket + label
y_max_ext <- y_max + 0.12 * range_y
# bracket position
p_y <- y_max + 0.05 * range_y


# Test & label 
wt <- wilcox.test(Distance ~ Comparison, data = OP_bal, exact = FALSE)
label <- ifelse(is.finite(wt$p.value) && wt$p.value < 0.001,
                "italic(p) < 0.001",
                paste0("italic(p) == ", sprintf('%.3f', wt$p.value)))

# Plot
OP_plot <- ggplot(OP_bal, aes(x = Comparison, y = Distance, fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = Comparison), width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Within individual" = "#7C674D", "Between individuals" = "#D2C6B7")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Within individual" = "#7C674D", "Between individuals" = "#D2C6B7")) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(y_min, y_max_ext), clip = "off") +

  labs(y = "Bray–Curtis Dissimilarity" ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  ggsignif::geom_signif(
    annotations = label, y_position = p_y,
    xmin = 1, xmax = 2, tip_length = 0.01, parse = TRUE
  )

ggsave("../Output/Longitudinal/Beta_diversity/OP_betweenvswithin_BC.svg",
       OP_plot, width = 5, height = 5)

```

## Combined plots NP and OP within vs between composition
```{r}
combined_plot <- NP_plot + OP_plot +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")

# Save combined plot
ggsave("../Output/Longitudinal/Beta_diversity/NPOP_betweenvswithin_BC.svg",
       combined_plot, width = 7, height = 3)
```


# Combine between vs within BC plots with delta lung function 

```{r}
combined_plot <- plot_delta / (NP_plot | OP_plot) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 2) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A")

# Save combined plot
ggsave("../Output/Longitudinal/Delta_lung_NPOP_betweenvswithin_BC.svg",
       combined_plot, width = 7, height = 7)
```
# Which taxa actually drive this stability over ~7 years?
# Correlation per taxon
```{r}
# What is a stable taxon? 

# A taxon is stable if:
# its abundance is similar within the same person at T0 and T1
# compared to variability across people.
# i.e. within-person variance < between-person variance

# Temporal stability was quantified using Spearman rank correlation coefficients (ρ) calculated between T0 and T1 abundances across individuals. Spearman correlation was chosen because microbiome abundance data are non-normally distributed and compositional, and rank-based measures are robust to outliers and non-linear relationships.
# A high positive correlation indicates that individuals maintained similar relative abundance rankings over time, consistent with long-term taxon stability, whereas correlations near zero indicate temporal variability or reshuffling of taxa among individuals.

# NP 
np_ps <- subset_samples(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly, niche == "NP")

# Prevalence filter function
filter_prevalence <- function(ps, prev = 0.1){
  mat <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if(taxa_are_rows(ps)) mat <- t(mat)
  keep <- colMeans(mat > 0) >= prev
  prune_taxa(keep, ps)
}

# 
filter_paired_taxa <- function(ps, min_pairs = 10){
  mat <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if(taxa_are_rows(ps)) mat <- t(mat)
  meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))
  paired_counts <- apply(mat > 0, 2, function(x){
    df <- data.frame(ID = meta$ID,
                     tp = meta$timepoint,
                     present = x)
    df %>%
      filter(present) %>%
      count(ID) %>%
      filter(n >= 2) %>%
      nrow()
  })

  prune_taxa(paired_counts >= min_pairs, ps)
}

VGO_NP_filtered <-
  VGO_NP_combined %>%
  filter_prevalence(0.1) %>% # ecological relevance - Kept taxa present in ≥10% of samples
  filter_paired_taxa(10) %>% # Kept taxa detected at both timepoints in ≥10 individuals.
  to_RA()

ntaxa(VGO_NP_filtered)# 24 taxa remaining after filters applied

otu <- as(otu_table(VGO_NP_filtered), "matrix")
if (taxa_are_rows(VGO_NP_filtered)) otu <- t(otu)

meta <- data.frame(sample_data(VGO_NP_filtered))

df <- as.data.frame(otu) %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID") %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("SampleID"),
            by="SampleID")

paired_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = colnames(otu),
    names_to = "Taxon",
    values_to = "Abundance"
  )

paired_wide <- paired_long %>%
  select(ID, timepoint, Taxon, Abundance) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = timepoint,
              values_from = Abundance) %>%
  filter(!is.na(T0) & !is.na(T1))

# compute a Spearman rank correlation across individuals - so this asks us whether individuals with high abundance at baseline also have relatively high abundance years later (7 years)
taxon_stability <- paired_wide %>%
  group_by(Taxon) %>%
  summarise(
    rho = cor(T0, T1, method = "spearman"),
    mean_abund = mean(c(T0, T1)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Plot the top 20 stable taxa
top_taxa <- taxon_stability %>%
  arrange(desc(rho)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)


# Taxon label formatter
format_taxon_labels <- function(labels) {

  sapply(labels, function(lab) {

    # replace underscores with spaces
    lab <- gsub("_", " ", lab)

    # detect trailing number
    if (grepl(" [0-9]+$", lab)) {

      text <- sub("([0-9]+)$", "", lab)
      num  <- sub(".* ([0-9]+)$", "\\1", lab)

      # italic taxon, number not italic
      bquote(italic(.(trimws(text))) ~ "(" * .(num) * ")")

    } else {

      bquote(italic(.(lab)))
    }
  })
}

# Make plot
top_corr_taxa_NP <- ggplot(top_taxa,
       aes(x = reorder(Taxon, rho), y = rho)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#7987af", alpha = 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = format_taxon_labels) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "T0–T1 Spearman correlation"  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)


ggsave( "../Output/Longitudinal/Correlation/top_corr_taxa_NP.svg", top_corr_taxa_NP, width = 5, height = 5)





# OP 
OP_ps <- subset_samples(ps_RQ2_pairedOnly, niche == "OP")

# Prevalence filter function
filter_prevalence <- function(ps, prev = 0.1){
  mat <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if(taxa_are_rows(ps)) mat <- t(mat)
  keep <- colMeans(mat > 0) >= prev
  prune_taxa(keep, ps)
}

# 
filter_paired_taxa <- function(ps, min_pairs = 10){
  mat <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix")
  if(taxa_are_rows(ps)) mat <- t(mat)
  meta <- data.frame(sample_data(ps))
  paired_counts <- apply(mat > 0, 2, function(x){
    df <- data.frame(ID = meta$ID,
                     tp = meta$timepoint,
                     present = x)
    df %>%
      filter(present) %>%
      count(ID) %>%
      filter(n >= 2) %>%
      nrow()
  })

  prune_taxa(paired_counts >= min_pairs, ps)
}

VGO_OP_filtered <-
  VGO_OP_combined %>%
  filter_prevalence(0.1) %>% # ecological relevance - Kept taxa present in ≥10% of samples
  filter_paired_taxa(10) %>% # Kept taxa detected at both timepoints in ≥10 individuals.
  to_RA()

ntaxa(VGO_OP_filtered)# 24 taxa remaining after filters applied

otu <- as(otu_table(VGO_OP_filtered), "matrix")
if (taxa_are_rows(VGO_OP_filtered)) otu <- t(otu)

meta <- data.frame(sample_data(VGO_OP_filtered))

df <- as.data.frame(otu) %>%
  rownames_to_column("SampleID") %>%
  left_join(meta %>% rownames_to_column("SampleID"),
            by="SampleID")

paired_long <- df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = colnames(otu),
    names_to = "Taxon",
    values_to = "Abundance"
  )

paired_wide <- paired_long %>%
  select(ID, timepoint, Taxon, Abundance) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = timepoint,
              values_from = Abundance) %>%
  filter(!is.na(T0) & !is.na(T1))

# compute a Spearman rank correlation across individuals - so this asks us whether individuals with high abundance at baseline also have relatively high abundance years later (7 years)
taxon_stability <- paired_wide %>%
  group_by(Taxon) %>%
  summarise(
    rho = cor(T0, T1, method = "spearman"),
    mean_abund = mean(c(T0, T1)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Plot the top 20 stable taxa
top_taxa <- taxon_stability %>%
  arrange(desc(rho)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

top_corr_taxa_OP <- ggplot(top_taxa,
       aes(x = reorder(Taxon, rho), y = rho)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#7b674d", alpha= 0.6) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = format_taxon_labels) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "T0–T1 Spearman correlation"  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)


ggsave( "../Output/Longitudinal/Correlation/top_corr_taxa_OP.svg", top_corr_taxa_OP, width = 5, height = 5)

```
